description:
## Goal
Publish `ModuleTypes::dead_branches` — the cross-spec consensus that a specific branch of a specific `Term::If` is provably never taken.

## Depends on
- E1 (no functional dependency, but the tag exists before the producer that downstream consumers will read alongside it).

## Shape
```rust
enum DeadBranch { Then, Else }
ModuleTypes {
    ...
    dead_branches: HashMap<(FnId, BlockId), DeadBranch>,
}
```

At most one branch per If can land here under the union (both-branches-dead means the If is unreachable; out of scope for this ticket, handled by block-level DCE).

## Producer
`ir_typer` after typing all specs. Reuse the existing `find_emptied_var` logic. For each `Term::If(cond, then_b, else_b)`:
- For each spec, narrow the operand on each branch and record whether the branch narrowed to `none`.
- A branch is `Dead` iff every live spec found it dead. (Same rule `collect_diagnostics` already uses for `unreachable-arm`.)
- Insert into `module_types.dead_branches`.

## Soundness
Cross-spec consensus is required. A branch dead under some specs and live under others is reachable in source (e.g. `sum`'s `[]` arm). Tests must include at least one fixture with multi-spec polymorphism (`sum`-style) to verify the join doesn't over-fire.

## Out of scope
- Consumers: E3 (diagnostic) and E4 (fold) will read this in their tickets.

## Done when
- `ModuleTypes::dead_branches` populated by `ir_typer::type_module`.
- Unit test: irrefutable destructure → entry yields `Else`. Refutable list pattern (typed `list(...)`) → no entry. Multi-spec polymorphic fn where branch is dead in some specs only → no entry.
- All existing tests pass.
title: E2: ModuleTypes::dead_branches — ir_typer publishes cross-spec consensus
